hero.title: What We Do
hero.description1: An initiative for the socio-economic integration of young orphans and those in precarious situations across Morocco.
hero.description2: Since 2021, FORSA & TAAHIL mentors, trains and integrates young people from orphanages, based on three pillars: mentoring, training and integration.

timeline.launch: Launch of the FORSA & TAAHIL program
timeline.launchDescription: Beginning of structured support for young people from orphanages.

program.subtitle: Integration Program
program.title: FORSA & TAAHIL Program
program.description: Inspired by the real needs on the ground, the program developed by the Al Amal Association aims at the socio-economic integration of young people from orphanages in Morocco, in partnership with public and private actors.

presentation.title: Presentation
presentation.description: Launched in January 2021, the FORSA & TAAHIL program supports young residents and former residents of orphanages through supervision, training and social and professional integration. It focuses on strengthening social autonomy and economic independence, with professional integration as a major pillar.

problematic.title: Problematic
problematic.description: Many young orphans, at 18, leave protection structures without sufficient support. Without a diploma or with an interrupted school career, they face great difficulties in entering the labor market and becoming active actors in society.

objective.title: Objective
objective.description: Contribute to the social and professional integration of young orphans and those in precarious situations in Morocco, by offering them structured support based on three essential pillars: Supervision, Training and Integration.

beneficiaries.title: The Young People We Support
beneficiaries.subtitle: we support
beneficiaries.description: The program targets very different profiles, but with the same objective: to offer each young person a clear path to autonomy, regardless of their starting point.

analysis.title: Analysis of Our Beneficiaries
analysis.lineChartTitle: Annual evolution of beneficiaries admitted by category

supervision.title: Supervision
supervision.description: Comprehensive and human support to help each young person build their life project and get out of precariousness.
details: Individual follow-up of young people from orphanages and social protection establishments
Orientation towards mechanisms adapted to each profile

training.title: Training
training.description: Training paths adapted to field realities to develop useful and quickly mobilizable skills.
details: Strengthening school skills for high school graduates
Development of soft skills: self-confidence, autonomy, communication

integration.title: Integration
integration.description: Social and economic integration as the ultimate goal: enabling each young person to become an actor in their future.
details: Connection with a wide network of public and private partners
Support towards employment, work-study or entrepreneurship
Follow-up after integration to secure pathways

bacheliers.title: High School Graduates
bacheliers.description: Young orphans who have reached the baccalaureate level benefit from comprehensive support until they obtain their diploma and pursue higher education.
details: Coverage of costs related to schooling and transport.
Regular pedagogical follow-up and support for success.

graduates.title: Graduates
graduates.description: Young orphans who are already graduates are supported towards employment thanks to a network of partners in many sectors.
details: Search for opportunities in companies (internships, jobs).
Coverage of transport, housing and sometimes food.

neet.title: NEET
neet.description: The largest category groups together young people without a diploma, without training, without employment, often in a situation of great precariousness.
details: Short-term training leading to a diploma or certificate.
Complete material support (food, transport, housing) until stabilization.
